steps.title: Use the semicircle arcs
steps.content:
Each diameter splits the circle into two semicircles. Since the circumference is $96\pi$, each semicircle has arc length
$$\frac{96\pi}{2}=48\pi$$

steps.title: Express the unknown arcs
steps.content:
Let arc $AB=x$. The problem gives arc $DA=3x$. Because $AC$ is a diameter,
$$AB+BC=48\pi$$
so arc $BC=48\pi-x$. Because $BD$ is a diameter, arc $CD=x$.

steps.title: Solve around the circle
steps.content:
The four arcs make the full circumference.
$$x+(48\pi-x)+x+3x=96\pi$$
$$4x+48\pi=96\pi$$
$$x=12\pi$$
